description: Ten unique email scenarios covering the PRD §7 primary use cases across a mix of tones and difficulty levels.

scenarios.title: Follow-up after client review meeting
scenarios.intent: Follow up after a client review meeting and confirm commitments
key_facts: Pilot rollout is scheduled for the week of May 12
A revised pricing sheet will be sent by Friday
Case studies will be shared on Thursday
We need confirmation on production data access by Monday

scenarios.title: Request for detailed proposal
scenarios.intent: Request a detailed proposal including scope, timeline, and pricing
key_facts: Proposal must cover migration of 12 services to the new platform
Target go-live is September 30
Budget ceiling is 180,000 USD
Proposal is due by April 30

scenarios.title: Reschedule a meeting
scenarios.intent: Ask to reschedule a previously confirmed meeting
key_facts: Original meeting was set for Thursday 10:00 UTC
Proposing Friday 14:00 UTC or Monday 09:00 UTC as alternatives
Need to preserve the same 45-minute duration

scenarios.title: Delay notification and apology
scenarios.intent: Notify the customer that a promised deliverable will be late and apologize
key_facts: Quarterly report was promised for April 12
New delivery date is April 18
Delay is due to a data pipeline recompute after a schema change
Offering a 30-minute walkthrough to compensate for the delay

scenarios.title: Escalate a production outage
scenarios.intent: Escalate an ongoing production outage and request an on-call engineer
key_facts: Checkout error rate spiked to 42% at 09:10 UTC
Incident is being coordinated in channel #inc-payments-042
Need an on-call SRE paged within 15 minutes
Last successful deploy was at 08:40 UTC

scenarios.title: Empathetic response to a dissatisfied client
scenarios.intent: Respond to a dissatisfied client with empathy and a concrete remediation plan
key_facts: Client reported three missed SLAs in March
We are assigning a dedicated customer success manager
A formal root-cause analysis will be delivered within 7 days
Next review is scheduled for April 22 at 15:00 UTC

scenarios.title: Internal status update for stakeholders
scenarios.intent: Provide a weekly internal status update to engineering stakeholders
key_facts: Onboarding service v2 reached feature complete
End-to-end tests are passing at 98%
Load testing is planned for next Tuesday
Security review is blocked on provider approval

scenarios.title: Thank-you email after a job interview
scenarios.intent: Thank the interview panel and reiterate interest in the role
key_facts: Interview was on April 14 with the platform team
Discussion focused on evaluation systems and prompt engineering
I am looking forward to the case-study next step
Available for a follow-up anytime this week

scenarios.title: Request approval on a PR/CR
scenarios.intent: Ask a reviewer for final approval on a change request
key_facts: Change request is CR-2187 in the release tracker
All required tests are green in CI run #4412
Deployment window closes on April 19 at 20:00 UTC
Two peers have already approved

scenarios.title: Clarification request on ambiguous requirements
scenarios.intent: Ask the product owner to clarify two ambiguous requirements before implementation begins
key_facts: Requirement R-7 specifies 'fast export' without a latency target
Requirement R-9 references 'supported regions' but no list is attached
Implementation is scheduled to start on April 22
A 20-minute sync is requested before Friday 17:00 UTC
